GlobalVariables.StudyDescription: Integrated data for efficacy and safety (including data from studies for efficacy: 21651, 21652; including data from Studies for safety: 21686, 21651, 21652, 21810)

ItemGroupDef.Name: ADAE
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per subject per each AE recorded in SDTM AE domain

ItemGroupDef.Name: ADCM
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per recorded medication occurrence or constant-dosing interval per subject recorded in SDTM CM domain

ItemGroupDef.Name: ADDS
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per disposition status or protocol milestone per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: ADEX
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One or more records per subject per analysis parameter

ItemGroupDef.Name: ADLB
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One or more records per subject per analysis parameter

ItemGroupDef.Name: ADMH
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per medical history event per subject recorded in SDTM MH domain

ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One or more records per subject per analysis parameter

ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One or more records per subject per analysis parameter

Description.TranslatedText: Derived from AE.AEENDTC. Missing end dates are imputed by replacing the missing information on the day and month of the event with the last day of the month and the last month of the year.
If AENDT is imputed, the following rules apply: If year is imputed set to 'Y', otherwise if month is imputed set to 'M', otherwise if day is imputed set to 'D'. If nothing was imputed keep empty.
Calculated as the number of days from reference start date to AENDT.
Concatenation of labels from AE.AESCONG, AE.AESDISAB, AE.AESDTH, AE.AESHOSP, AE.AESLIFE, AE.AESMIE and AE.AESONGFU (with comma), if variable contains Y.
Set 'Week 1-12' or 'Week 13-52' based on the Adverse event start date and treatment phase start and end date from ADSL.
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of ASTDT, AENDT, AETERM and AESEQ.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Derived from AE.AESEV. Set 1 if Mild, 2 if Moderate and 3 if Severe.
Set to 'Y' if the following AEs are included: 1. Potential AESI - Liver event, 2.Somnolence or fatigue, 3. Phototoxicity, 4. Post-menopausal uterine bleeding.

Derived from AE.AESTDTC. Incomplete dates will be imputed using the following rules: If the active treatment start date falls within imputation range, incomplete AE start date will be imputed as the active treatment start date. If the active treatment start date does not fall within the imputation range, and the placebo treatment start date falls within the imputation range, an incomplete AE start date is imputed as the placebo treatment start date. Otherwise, the partially missing AE start date will be imputed to the earliest date of the imputation range. Completely missing start date will not be imputed.
If ASTDT is imputed, the following rules apply: If year is imputed set to 'Y', otherwise if month is imputed set to 'M', otherwise if day is imputed set to 'D'. If nothing was imputed keep empty.
Calculated as the number of days from reference start date to ASTDT.

Set equal to '1', if AESI: Any condition potentially triggering close liver observation: SMQ Cholestasis and jaundice of hepatic organ SMQ Drug related hepatic disorders - severe events only SMQ Liver related investigations, signs and symptoms SMQ Liver-related coagulation and bleeding disturbances, included in this CQ. In addition, include AESIs ticked at AE-CRF.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable

Set equal to '2', if A) Somnolence MLG: PT Somnolence PT Hypersomnia B) Fatigue (MGL Decreased general strength and energy) PT Asthenia PT Decreased activity PT Fatigue PT Fatigue management PT Mental fatigue PT Physical deconditioning PT Sluggisheness In addition to MLGs above, include PT Sleep disorder due to general medical condition, hypersomnia type, included in this CQ.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Set equal to '3', if BMQ Photosensitivity reactions, included in this CQ.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable

Set equal to '4', if MLG Female genital tract bleeding PT Abnormal uterine bleeding PT Abnormal withdrawal bleeding PT Cervix haematoma uterine PT Cervix haemorrhage uterine PT Coital bleeding PT Haematocoele female PT Haematosalpinx PT Haemorrhagic ovarian cyst PT Ovarian haematoma PT Ovarian haemorrhage PT postmenopausal haemorrhage PT Uterine haematoma PT Uterine haemorrhage PT Vaginal haematoma PT Vaginal haemorrhage PT Vulval haematoma PT Vulval haematoma evacuation PT Vaginal haemorrhage PT Withdrawal bleed PT Unexpected vaginal bleeding on hormonal IUD, included in this CQ.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable

The customized query (CQ) name or name of the AE of special interest category based on a grouping of MedDRA terms. Would be blank for terms that are not in the CQ. Derivation is described in SAP 5.1 Coding conditions applicable for AESIs and also in TLF specification as footnotes: cq01cd for Potential AESI - Liver event, cq02cd for Somnolence or fatigue, cq03cd for Phototoxicity, cq04cd for Post-menopausal uterine bleeding, cq05cd for Abuse potential, cq06cd for Accidents and injuries, cq07cd for Postural hypotension, falls, black outs, syncope, dizziness, ataxia, fractures.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable

The customized query (CQ) name or name of the AE of special interest category based on a grouping of MedDRA terms. Would be blank for terms that are not in the CQ. Derivation is described in SAP 5.1 Coding conditions applicable for AESIs and also in TLF specification as footnotes: cq01cd for Potential AESI - Liver event, cq02cd for Somnolence or fatigue, cq03cd for Phototoxicity, cq04cd for Post-menopausal uterine bleeding, cq05cd for Abuse potential, cq06cd for Accidents and injuries, cq07cd for Postural hypotension, falls, black outs, syncope, dizziness, ataxia, fractures.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable

The customized query (CQ) name or name of the AE of special interest category based on a grouping of MedDRA terms. Would be blank for terms that are not in the CQ. Derivation is described in SAP 5.1 Coding conditions applicable for AESIs and also in TLF specification as footnotes: cq01cd for Potential AESI - Liver event, cq02cd for Somnolence or fatigue, cq03cd for Phototoxicity, cq04cd for Post-menopausal uterine bleeding, cq05cd for Abuse potential, cq06cd for Accidents and injuries, cq07cd for Postural hypotension, falls, black outs, syncope, dizziness, ataxia, fractures.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Post-treatment adverse events are defined as all adverse events starting after the date of the last treatment + 14 days.
If ASTDT < ADSL.TRTSDT then populate PREFL with a 'Y'.
Set to 'Y' if any adverse event occurring or worsening on or after the date of the first dose of study drug up to 14 days after the date of the last dose of study drug.
Derived from CM.CMENDTC. Missing end dates are imputed by replacing the missing information on the day and month of the event with the last day of the month and the last month of the year.
If AENDT is imputed, the following rules apply: If year is imputed set to Y, otherwise if month is imputed set to M, otherwise if day is imputed set to D. If nothing was imputed keep empty.
Derived from randomization date until AENDT.
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of CMCAT, ASTDT, CMENRTPT, AENDT, CMTRT and CMSEQ.

Derived from CM.CMSTDTC. Incomplete dates will be imputed using the following rules: If the active treatment start date falls within imputation range, incomplete CM start date will be imputed as the active treatment start date. If the active treatment start date does not fall within the imputation range, and the placebo treatment start date falls within the imputation range, an incomplete CM start date is imputed as the placebo treatment start date. Otherwise, the partially missing CM start date will be imputed to the earliest date of the imputation range. Completely missing start date will not be imputed.
If ASTDT is imputed, the following rules apply: If year is imputed set to Y, otherwise if month is imputed set to M, otherwise if day is imputed set to D. If nothing was imputed keep empty.
Derived from randomization date until ASTDT.

Set to 'Y' if start of medication is after last study drug intake.
Set to 'Y' if PROHIBFL='Y' and CM start occurred from randomization day up to and including day 82.

Only filled for records with ICE01FL = 'Y'. Contains the number of the last week flagged for the intercurrent event. First the end date of impact on efficacy is calculated as AENDY plus impact duration from preliminary list of prohibited concomitant medication in OASIS-1 and OASIS-2 SAPs section 6.5.3. If this end date occurs on and after the 3rd day of the week, the respective week will be flagged. If the end date for the impact on efficacy occurs on the 1st or 2nd day of the week, the respective week should not be flagged and only the previous week will be flagged. If intake of CM is ongoing, the value will be missing.

Only filled for intercurrent events (ICE) (records with ICE01FL = 'Y'). The value is derived from ASTDY and contains the number of the first week flagged for the ICE. If the ICE occurs on day 1, then ICESTWK=0. Otherwise, if the ICE occurs on or before the 5th day of the week, it will be flagged for the respective week. If the ICE occurs on the 6th or 7th day of the week, it will be flagged from the following week.

For SWITCH-1, OASIS1, 2, set to Y if (PH01SDT <= ASTDT <= min(PH01EDT, TRTEDT)) or (ASTDT < PH01SDT and (PH01SDT <= AENDT or missing(AENDT))), else set to missing. For OASIS3, set to Y if (PH01SDT <= ASTDT <= PH1SDT + 83) or (ASTDT < PH01SDT and (PH01SDT <= AENDT or missing(AENDT))), else set to missing.

For SWITCH-1, OASIS1, 2, set to Y if (PH02SDT <= ASTDT <= min(PH02EDT, TRTEDT)) or (ASTDT < PH02SDT and (PH02SDT <= AENDT or missing(AENDT))), else set to missing. For OASIS3, set to Y if (PH1SDT + 84 <= ASTDT <= min(PH2EDT, TRTEDT)) OR (ASTDT < min(PH1SDT + 84, TRTEDT) AND (PH1SDT + 84 <= AENDT OR MISSING(AENDT))), else set to missing.
For SWITCH-1, OASIS1, 2, ONTR03FL = ONTR02FL. For OASIS3, if ASTDT > PH1SDT + 181 THEN ONTR03FL = '', ELSE ONTR03FL = ONTR02FL.
Set to 'Y' if Medication taken before start of the study drug intake, (regardless of when it ended).

Set to 'Y' if any prohibited concomitant medication as defined in the protocol were used that influence efficacy. Please refer to OASIS-1 and OASIS-2 SAPs section 6.5.3 (Intake of prohibited concomitant medication having impact on efficacy) for more details.

Set to 'Y' if any VMS treatment as defined in the protocol were used that influence efficacy. Please refer to OASIS 1 and OASIS 2 SAPs section 6.5.2 (Permanent discontinuation of randomized treatment) for more details.
Treatment phase like 'Week 1-12' and 'Week 13-26' are determined based on the exposure start/ end date and treatment phase start and end date from ADSL, as per study schema.
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of ASTDT, DSCAT, DSSCAT, EPOCH, DSDECOD, DSTERM and DSSEQ.

If the subjects undergo a treatment phase and the subcategory for the disposition event is not "INFORMED CONSENT," then the ASTDT value will be equal to ADSL.TRTEDT. Otherwise, the ASTDT value will be the same as DS.DSSTDTC in Date 9 format.
Calculated as the number of days from Randomization date(ADSL.RANDDT) to ASTDT.
Set to 'Y' if a subject is randomized and permanently discontinued from study drug on or before day 82; otherwise set to missing.
Set to ADDS.DSDECOD in proper case where ADDS.ICE01FL is "Y"

If ICE01FL = 'Y' and subjects who received alternative VMS medication while on Treatment or after end of treatment ICESREAS= 'For participants who initiate alternative VMS treatment', otherwise if ICE01FL = 'Y' and no alternative medication was taken after treatment start then ICESREAS= 'For participants who remained untreated/on background therapy'.

If the ICE occurs on or before the 5th day of the week, it will be populated from the respective week If the ICE occurs on the 6th or 7th day of the week, it will be populated from the following week. If subject discontinue on day 1 then ICESTWK = 0. For example, if the participant permanently discontinues from the randomized drug on the 2nd day of week 4, then ICESTWK = 4. If the discontinuation from the randomized drug occurs on the 6th day of week 4, then ICESTWK = 5.
Derived from treatment end date if PARAMCD="TRT" or PARAMCD ="DURWDR" or PARAMCD="DURWGR" or PARAMCD="DURW".
If AENDT is imputed, the following rules apply: If year is imputed set to Y, otherwise if month is imputed set to M, otherwise if day is imputed set to D. If nothing was imputed keep empty.
Calculated as the number of days from reference start date in ADSL to AENDT.
Derived from the time portion of ADEX.EXENDTC and imputation rules defined in SAP.
Set to 1 for first drug period, set to 2 for second drug period, set to missing for RUN-IN epoch.

Treatment phase like 'Week 1-12' and 'Week 13-26' are determined based on the exposure start/ end date and treatment phase start and end date from ADSL, as per study schema. Treatment phase like 'Week 1-52' and 'Overall' are determined based on the day of first study drug intake up to and including the day of last study drug intake. For SWITCH-1, OASIS1 and OASIS2, treatment phase like 'Week 1-26' are determined based on the day of first study drug intake up to and including the day of last study drug intake. For OASIS3, treatment phase like 'Week 1-26' are determined based on the day of first study drug intake up to day 182.
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of PARAMCD, APHASE and ASTDT

Derived from EX.EXSTDTC for collected data when PARAMCD="TRT"; set to treatment start date when PARAMCD ="DURWDR" or PARAMCD="DURWGR" or PARAMCD="DURW"; else for intercurrent event parameters, set to the date of temporary interruption starting to impact efficacy.
If ASTDT is imputed, the following rules apply: If year is imputed set to Y, otherwise if month is imputed set to M, otherwise if day is imputed set to D. If nothing was imputed keep empty.
Calculated as the number of days from randomization date to ASTDT.
Derived from the time portion of ADEX.EXSTDTC and imputation rules defined in SAP.
Derived from EX.EXDOSE where PARAMCD = 'TRT' and Contains calculation results for PARAMCD not equal to 'TRT'.
Derived; contains calculation results for PARAM about intercurrent event.

For PARAMCD=COMPLPRC, categorized AVAL: < 80%,>= 80 to 120%,> 120%; for PARAMCD=DURWCRF and DURWDRY, APHASE=Overall, categorized AVAL: < 4 weeks, 4 - < 12 weeks, 12 - < 23 weeks, 23 - < 52 weeks, >= 52 weeks.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Set to 'Y' when the temporary treatment interruption occurred, otherwise blank.

Concatenate all the unique EC.ECADJ within the week window alphabetically and separate by comma; If no EC.ECADJ is available, set to "< 5/7 DAYS", "< 80% COMPLIANCE" or "< 80% Compliance and < 5/7 days" whichever apply.
For records taken over from SDTM.EX, PARAMCD is populated with 'TRT'. For other derived parameters, use the relevant term from global codelist.
If a new parameter is calculated, then populate as "DERIVED"; otherwise blank.
Derived based on the source data of the parameters. If from CRF then set to 'by eCRF', otherwise if from eDiary data set to 'by eDiary'.

Derived from ADEX.PARAMCD and ADEX.TRTAN. If PARAMCD in ("CMPCRFDR", "CMPCRFGR", "CMPDRYDR", "CMPDRYGR", "DURWDR", "DURWGR") and non-missing TRTAN, Set to 'by study drug'; PARAMCD in ("CMPCRFDR", "CMPCRFGR", "CMPDRYDR", "CMPDRYGR", "DURWDR", "DURWGR") and missing TRTAN, Set to 'by treatment group'; Otherwise set to missing.
Derived based on the purpose of the parameters. If for efficacy analysis purpose then set to 'Efficacy', otherwise if for safety analysis purpose set to 'Safety'.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Derived based on ADSL.TRT01AN, ADSL.TRT02AN and APHASE if PARAMCD in ("DURWDR", "DURWGR", "DURW", 'COMPLPRC', 'COMPLPRC', 'TOTDOSE').
For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2, based on the ePRO diary data, if treatment was taken less than 5 out of 7 days during day 2-8, set to Y; else set to N.

For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2, based on the ePRO diary data, if treatment was taken less than 5 out of 7 days during any 7-consecutive days of day 15-28 or 100 * Number of capsules taken / 56 < 80% during day 1-28, set to Y; else set to N.
For SWITCH-1, OASIS 1, 2 and 3, calculated as total amount of dose (from eDiary) / duration of treatment (in days, from CRF).

For SWITCH-1, calculated per study drug as 100 * number of capsules taken (from eDiary) / (treatment duration from CRF* 4) during the relevant window. For OASIS 1, 2 and 3, calculated per study drug as 100 * number of capsules taken (from eDiary) / (treatment duration from CRF* 2) during the relevant window.
For SWITCH-1, OASIS 1, 2 and 3, sum up amount of dose (from eDiary) during the relevant duration of treatment.
For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2 study, calculated per study drug as 100 * number of capsules taken (from eDiary) / (treatment duration from CRF* 2) during the relevant window.

For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2, based on the ePRO diary data, if treatment was taken less than 5 out of 7 days during any 7-consecutive days of day 71-84 or 100 * Number of capsules taken / 168 < 80% during day 1-84, set to Y; else set to N.

For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2, based on the ePRO diary data, if treatment was taken less than 5 out of 7 days during any 7-consecutive days of day 43-56 or 100 * Number of capsules taken / 112 < 80% during day 1-56, set to Y; else set to N.
For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2, calculated as total amount of dose (from CRF) / duration of treatment (in days, from CRF).
For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2, sum up amount of dose (from CRF) during the relevant duration of treatment.

For SWITCH-1, OASIS 1, 2 and 3, calculated per study drug the number of days from the day of first study drug intake up to and including the day of last study drug intake. For OASIS 1 and 2, Week 1-12 represents the placebo controlled period of the study and Week 13-26 represents the period at which all participants are treated with elinzanetant. For OASIS 3, Week 1-12 is defined from start of study treatment until (T3 visit date -1) and Week 13-52 as T3 visit date onwards until end of study treatment intake. Week 1-26 is defined from start of study treatment until day 182.

For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2, calculated per study drug as the number of weeks from the day of first study drug intake up to and including the day of last study drug intake for overall window. For Week 1-12, the treatment duration ends on the end of treatment phase 1, or on the day of last study drug intake, whatever is earlier. For Week 13-26, the treatment duration starts on the first day of treatment phase 2.
For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2, calculated per study drug as 100 * number of capsules taken (from CRF) / (treatment duration from CRF* 2) during the relevant window.

For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2, calculated per treatment group as the number of weeks from the day of first study drug intake up to and including the day of last study drug intake for overall window. For Week 1-12, the treatment duration ends on the end of treatment phase 1, or on the day of last study drug intake, whatever is earlier. For Week 13-26, the treatment duration starts on the first day of treatment phase 2.
For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2, calculated as total amount of dose (from eDiary) / duration of treatment (in days, from CRF).
For OASIS 1 and OASIS 2, sum up amount of dose (from eDiary) during the relevant duration of treatment.

Set to 'Y' for the latest available valid measurement at or before the start-of-treatment(for treated subjects) / randomization date (for randomized but not treated subjects). If the last observation available prior to randomization is the measurement from the Screening Visit, this would be used as the baseline value excluding unscheduled measurements.

Derived from LB.LBDTC date part. Missing start dates are imputed by replacing the missing information on the day and month of the event with the first day of the month and the first month of the year.
Derived based on ADT and randomization date plus 1.

Set to Y where any of the following is true: 1)Any CRIT flag is set to Y and the visit is unscheduled and the original visit is not a baseline visit and ADY > 1; 2)Visit is baseline or a scheduled post-baseline visit (in case of duplicate records by same parameter and analysis visit, only first is flagged) 3) Measurement is flagged as treatment emergent. For EOT visit, flagged for subjects of OASIS studies who took any Elinzanetant and prematurely discontinued study drug, for FU visit is flagged for subjects who took any Elinzanetant.
Set to Y where any of the following is true: 1)Double-blind/phase 1 scheduled visits; 2)FU visit; 3) EOT visit happened in Phase 1.
Set to Y where Scheduled visits at open label/phase 2 for subjects who switch from placebo to Elinzanetant or ABLFL=Y 2)Visit is an EOT/FU visit if happened after PH2SDT

Post-baseline measurements by week 1-12, Set to Y where all of the following are true: 1) For SWITCH-1, all measurements after first treatment dose date 2) For OASIS-1/2 a) all measurements after first treatment dose date but before or at PH2SDT b) if missing PH2SDT, all measurements after first treatment dose date 3) For OASIS-3 subjects with visit "week 12/14" available: all measurements after TRTSDT but before or at "week 12/14" visit date. For OASIS-3 subjects (non-completers) with visit "week 12/14" NOT available: a) If no scheduled visit after visit "week 12/14" available (except "End of Treatment"/ "Follow-up"), all measurements after TRTSDT b) if "week 12/14" visit is missing but visit "week 16/18" is available, then measurements after TRTSDT but before "week 16/18" visit date are included. All studies: measurements at TRTSDT but not treated as baseline (according to visit) are included.
Set to Y for switchers from OASIS-1/2 where both of the following are true: 1)Actual arm is "Placebo - Elinzanetant 120mg" 2) visit happened in phase 2
Flagging post-baseline measurements by week 1-26. Set to Y when ANL07FL is Y for SWITCH- and, OASIS-1/2. For OASIS-3 set Y when ANL07FL is Y and day of measurement is before or at study day 182.

Set to Y where either of the following is true: 1)For OASIS-1/2: a) If start Phase 1 with EZN, all visits after first treatment dose date; b) If start Phase 1 with Placebo and gone to Phase 2, visit is in Phase 2 and analysis date is after Phase 2 start date 2)Study is SWITCH-1 or OASIS-3 and analysis date is after treatment start date

Derive based on ADLB.AVAL and ADLB.ANRLO/ANRHI, if AAVAL in-between ANRLO and ANRHI then set 'NORMAL', if AVAL greater than ANRHI then set 'HIGH' if AVAL less than ANRLO then set 'LOW'. Otherwise set to LB.LBNRIND.
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of PARCAT1, PARAMN, AVISITN, ADT, ATM and LBSEQ.
Derived from time portion of LB.LBDTC.

Derived from LB.LBSTRESN and LB.LBSTRESC. For laboratory values with '<' sign, half the value of the LB.LBSTRESC will be used for analysis. For values with '>' sign, the LB.LBSTRESC will be used for analysis.
Equal to Derived from AVAL and LB.LBSTRESC
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable

Derived from LB.VISITNUM. Remap Screening visit to baseline if used as baseline, for derived parameter set avisitn as 600020 (post-baseline). For collected baseline safety data, if the date is after randomization date, set to 'Unscheduled'. Otherwise based on Z_AVISIT.
Derived from ADLB.AVAL by ADLB.USUBJID and ADLB.PARAMCD where ADLB.ABLFL='Y'.
Derived as AVAL-BASE, available for baseline and post-baseline records. Otherwise set to missing.
Set to '>=20 x ULN' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 20 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to '<3x ULN' if AVAL is less than 3 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to 'Y' if aval is less than 3 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to '>=1.5' if aval is greater than or equal to 1.5.
Set to 'Y' if aval is greater than or equal to 1.5.
Set to '>=2' if aval is greater than or equal to 2.
Set to 'Y' if aval is greater than or equal to 2.
Set to '>=2 x BL' if aval is greater than or equal to 2 times Baseline.
Set to 'Y' if aval is greater than or equal to 2 times Baseline.
Set to '>=3 x BL' if aval is greater than or equal to 3 times Baseline.
Set to 'Y' if aval is greater than or equal to 3 times Baseline.
Set to '>=5 x BL' if aval is greater than or equal to 5 times Baseline.
Set to 'Y' if aval is greater than or equal to 5 times Baseline.
Set to 'Y' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 20 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to '>=10 x ULN' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 10 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to 'Y' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 10 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to '>=8x ULN' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 8 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to 'Y' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 8 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to '>=5x ULN' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 5 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to 'Y' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 5 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to '>=3x ULN' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 3 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to 'Y' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 3 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to '>=2x ULN' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 2 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to 'Y' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 2 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to '>=1.5x ULN' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 1.5 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to 'Y' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 1.5 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to '>=1x ULN' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 1 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to 'Y' if AVAL is greater than or equal to 1 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to '<2x ULN' if aval is less than 2 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Set to 'Y' if aval is less than 2 times Normal Range Upper Limit.
Numeric representation of PARAMCD based on combination LB.LBTEST/ LB.LBTESTCD, LB.LBSTRESU, LB.LBSPEC, LB.LBMETHOD and any other variables needed for unique description.
If a new parameter is calculated, then populate as "DERIVED"; otherwise blank.
Derived from LB.LBSCAT. Keep PARCAT2 blank if LBSCAT eq 'CLOSE LIVER OBSERVATION'

Set to 'Y' if ADLB.ANRIND worsening: SWITCH-1: Post-baseline measurements up to TRTEDT+14; OASIS-1/2: 1) Subjects who started open-label phase: post-baseline, excluding measurements after the start day of open-label phase, 2) Subjects who did not start open label: post-baseline measurements up to TRTEDT+14. OASIS-3: 1) Subjects not discontinuing before visit "week 12/14": post-baseline, excluding measurements after visit "week 12/14" 2) Subjects who discontinued before week 12: post-baseline measurements up to day 84 or the day of TRTEDT+14.
Set to 'Y' if ADLB.ANRIND worsening: OASIS-1/2 switchers, measurements after PH2SDT and up to TRTEDT+14.
Derived Treatment Emergent of laboratory data. Set to 'Y' if ADLB.ANRIND worsening on or after the date of the first dose of study drug up to 14 days after the date of the last dose of study drug.
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALT or AST 3 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit and Total bilirubin 1.5 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit Otherwise Set 'N'.
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALT or AST 3 times greater than or equal to Baseline value Otherwise Set 'N'.
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALT or AST 5 times greater than or equal to Baseline value Otherwise Set 'N'.
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALT or AST 2 times greater than or equal to Baseline value and Total bilirubin 2 times greater than or equal to Baseline value Otherwise Set 'N'.
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALP 3 times Baseline value and Total bilirubin 2 times Baseline value Otherwise Set 'N'.
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALT or AST 3 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit and Total bilirubin 2 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit Otherwise Set 'N'.

Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALT or AST 3 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit and Total bilirubin 2 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit within 30 days afterwards. Otherwise Set 'N'.
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALT or AST 3 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit and INR greater than or equal to 1.5 Otherwise Set 'N'
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALT or AST 5 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit for more than 2 weeks.
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALT or AST 8 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit Otherwise Set 'N'.
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALT or AST 3 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit and with appearance of clinical signs/symptoms* in CE.CETERM. Otherwise Set 'N'.
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALP 2 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit and Total bilirubin 2 times greater than or equal to Normal Range Upper Limit Otherwise Set 'N'.
Set AVALC eq 'Y' if ALT or AST 3 times greater than or equal to Baseline value and Total bilirubin 2 times greater than or equal to Baseline value Otherwise Set 'N'.
Derived from MH.MHENDTC. Incomplete or missing dates are not imputed.
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of MHSEQ.
Derived from MH.MHSTDTC. Incomplete or missing dates are not imputed.
Flag last observation before treatment for all derived values and one non-derived value EQVAS score in "EQ-5D-5L" category.
Derived based on ADT and randomization date plus 1.
Set to 'Y' if record is on or after the week of permanent discontinuation of randomized treatment (ADDS.ICESTWK when ADDS.ICE01FL='Y') until week 12.
Set to 'Y' if record is on the same week of temporary treatment interruption (ADEX.PARAMCD is 'TRTINW1', 'TRTINW4', 'TRTINW8' or 'TRTINW12' when ADEX.ICE01FL='Y').

Set to 'Y' if record is during the effective period for intake of prohibited concomitant medication having impact on efficacy. The start and end of period can be identified by ADCM.ICESTWK and ADCM.ICEENWK when ADCM.ICE01FL='Y'.

For PROMIS, MENQOL, PGI, BDI, ISI and EQ-5D-5L, set to 'Y' if record is the highest priority day of the week window of each AVISITN and need to be displayed in descriptive table. For non-derived PARAMCD in C-SSRS BASELINE/SCREENING category, set to 'Y' for the last available records with outcome value is not 'INCOMPLETE'. For non-derived PARAMCD in C-SSRS SINCE LAST VISIT category, set to 'Y' for the first available records with outcome value is not 'INCOMPLETE'. For PARAMCD in HISTORY OF MENOPAUSE HORMONE THERAPY BAYER V1.0, set to 'Y' for the last available record.

Set to 'Y' if record is during the effective period for intake of VMS treatment having impact on efficacy. The start and end of period can be identified by ADCM.ICESTWK and ADCM.ICEENWK when ADCM.ICE01FL='Y'.
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of PARCAT1, PARCAT2, PARAMCD, ADT, AVISITN and QSSEQ.
Derived from time portion of QS.QSDTC.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Derived from QS.QSSTRESN for records for non-derived parameters; for derived parameters, refer to AVAL metadata.
Derived based on the description information obtained from QS.QSORRES for non-derived parameters. Set to null for derived parameters.

Derived categorized total score of BDI-II when PARCAT1= 'BECK DEPRESSION INVENTORY (BDI)', 0-13: none to minimal depression, 14-19: mild depression, 20-28: moderate depression, 29-63: severe depression. Derived categorized total score of ISI when PARCAT1= 'ISI', 0-7: No insomnia, 8-14: Subthreshold insomnia, 15-21: Clinical insomnia (moderate severity), 22-28: Clinical insomnia (severe).
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
AVISITN will be assigned based on highest ADY priority of the week window.
Derived from ADQS.AVAL by ADQS.USUBJID and ADQS.PARAMCD where ADQS.ABLFL="Y".
Derived as AVAL-BASE, available for baseline and post-baseline records. Otherwise set to missing.

Set to 'LOCF' if record is week 4 duplicated from week 2 or week 3. Set 'COPY' if records is scheduled week duplicated from follow-up record or follow-up record duplicated from record closest to target follow-up date.
Reason for permanent discontinuation of randomized treatment from ADDS.ICEREAS.
Reason for temporary treatment interruption from ADEX.ICEREAS.
If a new parameter is calculated, then populate as "DERIVED"; otherwise blank.
Derived from QS.QSCAT for all the records.
Derived from QS.QSSCAT for all the records.
Derived as (AVAL-BASE)*100/BASE, available for baseline and post-baseline records. Otherwise set to missing.

For derived-parameter non-missing baseline record, set to Y.
Derived from QS.QSDTC for non-derived parameters; Set to theoretical end diary date of the analysis week for derived parameters.
Derived based on ADT and randomization date.
Set to 'Y' if record is on or after the week of permanent discontinuation of randomized treatment (ADDS.ICESTWK when ADDS.ICE01FL='Y') until week 12.
Set to 'Y' if record is on the same week of temporary treatment interruption (ADEX.PARAMCD is 'TRTINW1', 'TRTINW4', 'TRTINW8' or 'TRTINW12' when ADEX.ICE01FL='Y').

Set to 'Y' if record is during the effective period for intake of prohibited concomitant medication having impact on efficacy. The start and end of period can be identified by ADCM.ICESTWK and ADCM.ICEENWK when ADCM.ICE01FL='Y'.
When PARAMTYP='DERIVED' and AVAL is not missing, set to 'Y' if it's a follow-up week or a scheduled week containing any day info on or before last dosing date.

Set to 'Y' if record is during the effective period for intake of VMS treatment having impact on efficacy. The start and end of period can be identified by ADCM.ICESTWK and ADCM.ICEENWK when ADCM.ICE01FL='Y'.
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of PARCAT1, ATPTN, PARAMCD, AVISITN, ADT and QSSEQ.
Derived from time portion of QS.QSDTC.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Based on QS.QSEVINTX if QSCAT= 'SLEEPINESS SCALE V1.0'; based on QS.QSSCAT if QSCAT= 'TWICE DAILY HOT FLASH DIARY V2.0'.
Derived based on QS.QSORRES for non-derived parameters; for derived parameters, refer to AVAL metadata.
Derived based on QS.QSORRES for non-derived parameters.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
AVISITN will be assigned based on week window for derived parameters.
Derived from ADQSHFSS.AVAL by ADQSHFSS.USUBJID and ADQSHFSS.PARAMCD where ADQSHFSS.ABLFL='Y'.
Derived as AVAL-BASE, available for baseline and post-baseline records. Otherwise set to missing.
Set to "At least 50% reduction in mean daily frequency" for mean daily frequency of moderate to severe hot flashes (PARAMCD=HFDB998).

For baseline and post-baseline records of mean daily frequency of moderate to severe hot flashes (PARAMCD=HFDB998), set to "Y" if CRIT1 is met; set to "N" if CRIT1 is not met but AVAL and BASE are not missing; otherwise set to blank.
Reason for permanent discontinuation of randomized treatment from ADDS.ICEREAS.
Reason for temporary treatment interruption from ADEX.ICEREAS.
If a new parameter is calculated, then populate as "DERIVED"; otherwise blank.
Derived from QS.QSCAT for all the records.
Derived as (AVAL-BASE)*100/BASE, available for baseline and post-baseline records. Otherwise set to missing.
Average morning item score during the week window (available diary days should be at least 11 out of 14 for baseline, and at least 5 out of 7 for treatment week).

For baseline: First calculate daily value for the available days as [(2 x number of moderate HF) + (3 x number of severe HF)] / (total number of moderate and severe hot flashes on that day). When no HF are reported for a particular day, set to 0. Then calculate the mean daily value by averaging the daily severity of HF of the available days during the week window. For post-baseline: First calculate daily value for the available days as [(1 x number of mild HF) + (2 x number of moderate HF) + (3 x number of severe HF)] / (total number of mild, moderate and severe hot flashes on that day). When no HF are reported for a particular day, set to 0. Then calculate the mean daily value by averaging the daily severity of HF of the available days during the week window (available diary days should be at least 11 out of 14 for baseline, and at least 5 out of 7 for treatment week).
Average afternoon item scores during the week window (available diary days should be at least 11 out of 14 for baseline, and at least 5 out of 7 for treatment week).
Average evening item scores during the week window (available diary days should be at least 11 out of 14 for baseline, and at least 5 out of 7 for treatment week).

First calculate daily value by averaging daily individual item scores (i.e., morning, afternoon and evening scores); Then calculate the mean daily value by averaging the daily value of the available days during the week window (available diary days should be at least 11 out of 14 for baseline, and at least 5 out of 7 for treatment week).

Total number of nighttime awakenings during the week window/ total number of available days during the week window (available diary days should be at least 11 out of 14 for baseline, and at least 5 out of 7 for treatment week).

Number of days with sleep disturbance due to HF rated as 'quite a bit' or 'very much' during the week window/ total number of available days with morning data during the week window (available diary days should be at least 11 out of 14 for baseline, and at least 5 out of 7 for treatment week).

Total number of mild, moderate, and severe HF during the week window / total number of available days with data during the week window (available diary days should be at least 11 out of 14 for baseline, and at least 5 out of 7 for treatment week).

Total number of moderate to severe HF during the week window/ total number of available days with data during the week window (available diary days should be at least 11 out of 14 for baseline, and at least 5 out of 7 for treatment week).
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of PARAMCD, AVISITN, ADT and RPSEQ.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Derived from ADSE.SEENDTC. If date parts are missing, imputation rules as defined in SAP are to be applied.
If AENDT is imputed, the following rules apply: If year is imputed set to Y, otherwise if month is imputed set to M, otherwise if day is imputed set to D. If nothing was imputed keep empty.
Derived from SE.SEENDTC considering the imputation rules defined in SAP.
Derived from the time portion of ADSE.SEENDTC and imputation rules defined in SAP.
If AENTM is imputed, the following rules apply: If hour is imputed set to H, otherwise if minute is imputed set to M, otherwise if second is imputed set to S. If nothing was imputed keep empty.
Derivation of treatment analysis period based on study
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of key variables.
Derived from ADSE.SESTDTC. If date parts are missing, imputation rules as defined in SAP are to be applied.
If ASTDT is imputed, the following rules apply: If year is imputed set to Y, otherwise if month is imputed set to M, otherwise if day is imputed set to D. If nothing was imputed keep empty.
Source and imputation rules are defined in SAP.
Derived from the time portion of ADSE.SESTDTC and imputation rules defined in SAP.
If ASTTM is imputed, the following rules apply: If hour is imputed set to H, otherwise if minute is imputed set to M, otherwise if second is imputed set to S. If nothing was imputed keep empty.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Categorized age:agegr1n <40 years, 40-49 years, 50-59 years, 60-65 years, >65 years.
Derived from AGE, SEX, RACE: A=Asian; B=Black; W=White; AI=American Indian or Alaska Native; NH=Native Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander; MUL=Multiple; NR=Not reported; OT=Other;
Derived from ADVS.AVAL where ABLFL='Y' and PARAMCD='BMI'
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Categorized BMI:<18.5, 18.5 to <25.0, 25.0 to <30, >= 30.0 kg/m2, Missing.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
This information is needed for Standard exposure Tables for the RMP. Since this information is not collected in any of these studies this variable is set to 'NOT APPLICABLE' for all subjects.
Y if subject has completed Treatment and FUP phase. Otherwise set to N

Derived from DS.DSDECOD when DS.DSCAT NE 'PROTOCOL MILESTONE' and DS.EPOCH not equal to 'SCREENING' andDS.dsdecod NOT IN ('COMPLETED','SCREEN FAILURE') and DS.DSSCAT not equal to 'INFORMED CONSENT' andDS.DSSUBDEC not in ('CONTINUE WITH SCHEDULED VISITS/ PROCEDURES' 'CONTINUE WITH PHONE CONTACTS REPLACING SITE VISITS').
Derived from DM.DTHDTC if all date parts (month, day, year) are given. Otherwise missing (blank).
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Derived by using B_EGFRN. Using the following ranges: < 25, 25 -< 45, 45 -< 60 and >= 60 mL/min/1.73m2

Set to "Y" if subject has observation in DS with dscat EQ "PROTOCOL MILESTONE" AND dsdecod EQ "INFORMED CONSENT OBTAINED" AND dsscat EQ "INFORMED CONSENT" AND NOT missing(DSSTDTC), otherwise set to "N".
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Set to 1 if any EX.EXSTDTC is not missing and epoch is not 'RUN-IN', otherwise set to 0.
Full Analysis Set (FAS) is defined as all randomized subjects
Derived from ADVS.AVAL where ABLFL='Y' and PARAMCD='HEIGHT'
Hepatic impairment is defined via SMQ hepatic disorders (excl. sub-SMQs Liver related investigations, signs and symptoms and Liver-related coagulation and bleeding disturbances)
Derived using DS.DSSTDTC where DSCAT EQ "PROTOCOL MILESTONE" AND DSDECOD EQ "DATE OF LAST VISIT"

OASIS-1&2: first day of drug 2 intake - 1 day (based on eCRF entry) if participants stayed in the study for at least 12 weeks and received the part two drug. For participants who have not received the part two drug due to early discontinuation of randomized treatment or study withdrawal: 1) set to week 12 assessment date or the post treatment phase end date, whichever is earlier, for participants who agreed to complete the assessments until week 12 or 2) study drug discontinuation date or EoT assessment date, whichever is later, for participants who decided to go to the follow up period or withdraw from the study. OASIS-3: end date of week 12 assessment -1 day is used. If week 12 assessment date is not available, end of treatment assessment date or date of last exposure to randomized treatment or end date of treatment period (non-completers), whichever is later, is used. SWITCH-1: date of last exposure to randomized treatment is used. All studies: For randomized but not treated subject use randomization date
Date of first exposure to treatment is used. If subject is randomized but not treated, then date of randomization is used.

OASIS-1&2: end of treatment period date is used for participants who completed the study. For participants who discontinue from treatment/study prematurely during the second phase the study discontinuation date or EoT assessment date, whichever is later, is used. OASIS-3: date of last exposure to randomized treatment is used for participants who completed the study. For participants who discontinue from treatment/study prematurely during the second phase, end of treatment visit date or date of last exposure to randomized treatment or end date of treatment period, whichever is later, is used.

OASIS-1&2: the first day of part 2 drug intake (identified based on the eCRF entry) is used. OASIS-3: phase 1 end date +1 is used in case week 12 assessment exist. SWITCH-1 does not have phase 2 as planned duration of randomized treatment is 12 weeks
Pregnant women are identified based on information through the urine test in the laboratory data or pelvic ultrasound performed during the treatment phase
Derived from DS.DSSTDTC where DSDECOD='RANDOMIZED' if all date parts (month, day, year) are given. Otherwise missing (blank).
Set to "Y" if subject was randomized and has a randomization number (DS.DSDECOD="RANDOMIZED" and SUPPDS.RANDNO is not null). Otherwise set to "N".
Derived from RANDNO, AGE, SEX, RACE: A=Asian; B=Black; W=White; AI=American Indian or Alaska Native; NH=Native Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander; MUL=Multiple; NR=Not reported; OT=Other;
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Categorized country: North America, the rest of the world.
Derived from DM.RFENDTC, if all date parts (month, day, year) are given. Otherwise missing (blank).
Derived from DM.RFICDTC, if all date parts (month, day, year) are given. Otherwise missing (blank).
Derived from DM.RFPENDTC, if all date parts (month, day, year) are given. Otherwise missing (blank).
Derived from DM.RFSTDTC if all date parts (month, day, year) are given. Otherwise missing (blank).
Derived from DM.RFXENDTC if all date parts (month, day, year) are given. Otherwise missing (blank).
Derived from DM.RFXSTDTC if all date parts (month, day, year) are given. Otherwise missing (blank).
Safety Analysis Set is defined as all participants who receive at least one dose of study drug.
Derived from SUBJID, AGE, SEX, RACE: A=Asian; B=Black; W=White; AI=American Indian or Alaska Native; NH=Native Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander; MUL=Multiple; NR=Not reported; OT=Other;
Set to "Y" if the subject was a screen failure (DM.ARMCD is "SCRNFAIL"). Otherwise set to "N".
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Derived from SU.SUNCF where SUCAT='TOBACCO'. Priority would be 'Current' > 'Former' > 'Never'.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01EDT, TR02EDT, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TRT01EDT should be populated. Derived from EX.EXENDTC for active treatment.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01EDTM, TR02EDTM, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TR01EDTM should be populated. Derived from EX.EXENDTC for active treatment.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01ETM, TR02ETM, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TRT01ETM should be populated. Derived from time portion of EX.EXENDTC for active treatment.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01SDT, TR02SDT, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TRT01SDT should be populated. Derived from EX.EXSTDTC for active treatment.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01SDTM, TR02SDTM, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TR01SDTM should be populated. Derived from EX.ESTNDTC for active treatment.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01STM, TR02STM, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TRT01STM should be populated. Derived from the time portion of EX.EXSTDTC for active treatment.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01EDT, TR02EDT, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TRT01EDT should be populated. Derived from EX.EXENDTC for active treatment.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01EDTM, TR02EDTM, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TR01EDTM should be populated. Derived from EX.EXENDTC for active treatment.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01ETM, TR02ETM, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TRT01ETM should be populated. Derived from time portion of EX.EXENDTC for active treatment.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01SDT, TR02SDT, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TRT01SDT should be populated. Derived from EX.EXSTDTC for active treatment.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01SDTM, TR02SDTM, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TR01SDTM should be populated. Derived from EX.ESTNDTC for active treatment.

In trial designs where multiple treatments are given, variables for each set of treatment dates must be defined as TR01STM, TR02STM, etc. In trial designs with only one treatment period only TRT01STM should be populated. Derived from the time portion of EX.EXSTDTC for active treatment.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable

Numeric derivation based on the subjects elements from SE domain. The treatment code is generated through the element code (ETCD) and the provided codelist Z_TRT. TRTxxAN should be mapped one-to-one mapping to TRTxxA within a study.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Numeric derivation based on the value of ARMCD for each respective period mapped via project specific informat Z_TRT. TRTxxPN should be mapped one-to-one mapping to TRTxxP within a study.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable

Numeric derivation based on the subjects elements from SP.SE. The treatment code is generated through the element code (ETCD) and the provided codelist Z_TRT. TRTxxAN should be mapped one-to-one mapping to TRTxxA within a study.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Numeric derivation based on the value of ARMCD for each respective period mapped via project specific informat Z_TRT. TRTxxPN should be mapped one-to-one mapping to TRTxxP within a study.
Derived from last EX.EXENDTC. If the date is not available and subject has a record with DS.EPOCH="TREATMENT" and DS.DSSCAT is missing, the last day of drug intake from eDiary will be used.
Derived from the very first EX.EXSTDTC if all date parts (month, day, year) are given and EPOCH is not RUN-IN. Otherwise missing (blank).
Derived from USUBJID, AGE, SEX, RACE: A=Asian; B=Black; W=White; AI=American Indian or Alaska Native; NH=Native Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander; MUL=Multiple; NR=Not reported; OT=Other;
Derived from ADVS.AVAL where ABLFL='Y' and PARAMCD='WEIGHT'.

Set to Y for all scheduled visits, if duplicated records exist for the same visit, for visit before baseline the last record is flagged, for visit after baseline the first record is flagged. For EOT visit flagged for subjects of OASIS studies who took any Elinzanetant and prematurely discontinued study drug, for FU visit is flagged for subjects who took any Elinzanetant.
Set to Y where any of the following is true: 1) Double-blind phase scheduled visits; 2) FU visit; 3) EOT visit happened in Phase 1.
Set to Y where 1) Scheduled visits at open label/phase 2 for subjects who switch from placebo to Elinzanetant or ABLFL=Y; 2) Visit is an Eot/FU visit if happened after PH2SDT
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Derived from SV.VISITNUM and SV.VISIT. Remapped based on Z_AVISIT.
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of PARAMCD, ADT.
Numeric analysis value described by PARAMCD.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Assigned per time interval: 121 means time to Week 12, 600000 means from week 12 to end study, 700000 means from study start to study end.
Set to 0 if an event of interest is observed; 1 if no event by observed window.
Describes the event of interest, or censoring reason for the subject within the parameter.
If a new parameter is calculated, then populate as "DERIVED"; otherwise blank.
The name of the SDTM domain (or dataset) or analysis dataset that relates to the analysis variable (e.g., AVAL).
The sequence number --SEQ or ASEQ of the row (in the domain or dataset identified by SRCDOM) that relates to the analysis variable.
The name of the column (in the domain or dataset identified by SRCDOM) that relates to the analysis variable.

Calculated as days from randomization to first occurrence of ALP>=3xULN; If no such an increase is observed, the observation is censored at the last visit date (if missing use end of participation date).If a participant does not have any post-baseline data, she will be censored at baseline.

Calculated as days from randomization to first occurrence of ALT>=3xULN; If no such an increase is observed, the observation is censored at the last visit date (if missing use end of participation date).If a participant does not have any post-baseline data, she will be censored at baseline.

Calculated as weeks from randomization to permanent discontinuation of randomized treatment; If event did not occur by day 84, the participant will be censored at week 12;For randomized but not treated subject, she had an event on randomization (week 0).

Calculated as weeks from randomization to first intake of prohibited concomitant medication having impact on efficacy; If event did not occur by day 84, the participant will be censored at week 12 or at the time of dropping out of the study,whichever occurs earlier.

Set to 'Y' for the latest available valid measurement at or before the start-of-treatment(for treated subjects) / randomization date (for randomized but not treated subjects). If the last observation available prior to randomization is the measurement from the Screening Visit, this would be used as the baseline value excluding unscheduled measurements.
Derived based on ADT and randomization date plus 1.

Set to Y for all scheduled visits, if duplicated records exist for the same visit, for visit before baseline the last record is flagged, for visit after baseline the first record is flagged. For EOT visit flagged for subjects of OASIS studies who took any Elinzanetant and prematurely discontinued study drug, for FU visit is flagged for subjects who took any Elinzanetant.
Set to Y where any of the following is true: 1)Double-blind/phase 1 scheduled visits; 2)FU visit; 3) Eot visit happened in Phase 1.
Set to Y where Scheduled visits at open label/phase 2 for subjects who switch from placebo to Elinzanetant or ABLFL=Y 2)Visit is an Eot/FU visit if happened after PH2SDT

Post-baseline measurements by week 1-12, Set to Y where all of the following are true: 1) For SWITCH-1, all measurements after first treatment dose date 2) For OASIS-1/2 a) all measurements after first treatment dose date but before or at PH2SDT b) if missing PH2SDT, all measurements after first treatment dose date 3) For OASIS-3 subjects with visit "week 12/14" available: all measurements after TRTSDT but before or at "week 12/14" visit date. For OASIS-3 subjects (non-completers) with visit "week 12/14" NOT available: a) If no scheduled visit after visit "week 12/14" available (except "End of Treatment"/ "Follow-up"), all measurements after TRTSDT b) if "week 12/14" visit is missing but visit "week 16/18" is available, then measurements after TRTSDT but before "week 16/18" visit date are included. All studies: measurements at TRTSDT but not treated as baseline (according to visit) are included.
Set to Y for switchers from OASIS-1/2 where both of the following are true: 1)Actual arm is "Placebo - Elinzanetant 120mg" 2)visit happened in phase 2
Flagging post-baseline measurements by week 1-26. Set to Y when ANL07FL is Y for SWITCH- and, OASIS-1/2. For OASIS-3 set Y when ANL07FL is Y and day of measurement is before or at study day 182.

Set to Y where either of the following is true: 1)For OASIS-1/2: a) If start Phase 1 with EZN, all visits after first treatment dose date; b) If start Phase 1 with Placebo and gone to Phase 2, visit is in Phase 2 and analysis date is after Phase 2 start date 2)Study is SWITCH-1 or OASIS-3 and analysis date is after treatment start date
Calculated sequence number starting with 1 and increasing by 1 within a subject in sort order of PARAMCD, AVISITN, ADT and VSSEQ.

Derived character string mapping from AVAL if both are present on a given record. In this case, the mapping must be one-to-one between AVAL and AVALC. Otherwise, if only AVALC is needed to describe the analysis value, will be taken from VS.VSSTRESC for records coming directly from SDTM. On a given record, it is permissible for AVAL, AVALC, or both to be null. AVAL is required if AVALC is not present, since either AVAL or AVALC must be present in the dataset. May also be calculated based on an algorithm or statistical method as dictated by analysis needs.
Decode variable of corresponding numeric variable
Derived from VS.VISITNUM. Remap Screening visit to baseline for baseline records otherwise based on Z_AVISIT.
Derived from ADVS.AVAL by ADVS.USUBJID and ADVS.PARAMCD where ADVS.ABLFL='Y'
Derived as AVAL-BASE, available for baseline and post-baseline records. Otherwise set to missing.
CRIT1: MAX POST BASELINE VALUE DURING PHASE 1
Flag which should be populated with "Y" when the criterion has been met.
CRIT2: MAX POST BASELINE VALUE DURING PHASE 2
Flag which should be populated with "Y" when the criterion has been met.
CRIT3: MAX POST BASELINE VALUE WEEK 1-26
Flag which should be populated with "Y" when the criterion has been met.
CRIT4: MAX POST BASELINE VALUE WEEK 1-52.
Flag which should be populated with "Y" when the criterion has been met.
New code based on combination of VS.VSTESTCD/ VS.VSTEST, VS.VSSTRESU and any other variables needed for unique description.
